rulings.Case Title: FRANK ALIOTO et al VS. WILLIAM F. GARLOCK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 2, 2012, Line 1, DEFENDANT HAMILTON, RICCI & ASSOCIATES, INC.'s Motion For Judgment is Granted. No opposition filed. The entire action is not automatically stayed due to the filing of a bankruptcy petition against Mr. Garlock. The stay of 11 U.S.C. 362 ordinarily does not automatically apply against non-debtor codefendants, such as moving party Hamilton, Ricci & Associates. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: PEGASTAFF, A DIVISION OF PEGASOFT CORPORATION, A VS. CORESTAFF SERVICES, LP, A DELAWARE LIMITED et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION To Compel Deft Pacific Gas & Electric Company'S Further Responses To Discovery; Proof Of Service
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 2, 2012, Line 1, PLAINTIFF PEGASTAFF'S MOTION To Compel Defendant Pacific Gas & Electric Company'S Further Responses To Discovery;-Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem has not issued a tentative ruling on this motion. Each party is required to bring a proposed order to the hearing. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: SHERRI WATSON VS. ROBERT G GISH M.D.
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Moiton By Robert G. Gish, M.D. To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 2, 2012, Line 2, DEFENDANT ROBERT GISH M.D. Notice Of Motion And Moiton By Robert G. Gish, M.D. To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: A Declaration of Non Opposition ("Plaintiff's Response to Defendants' Motions to Compel and Declaration of Jeffrey C. Bogert [No Opposition]") dated March 15, 2012 was apparently filed by Plaintiff's counsel. The Court does not have a courtesy copy of the executed Declaration or proof of service, but based on an electronic copy of same sent by Plaintiff's counsel has no reason to doubt that the Declaration is genuine. If Defendants dispute the Declaration of Non Opposition, they should appear. Otherwise the matter is off-calendar. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: KATHERINE D. CHAMBERLIN VS. WALGREEN CO., AN ILLINOIS CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Prelim Approval Of Class Settlement
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 2, 2012, Line 5, PLAINTIFF KATHERINE CHAMBERLIN'S Motion For Preliminary Approval Of Class Settlement is Denied. The settlement provides very little, if any, real value to the class because members of the class are unlikely to know of their right to redeem the gift cards for cash. This concern might be rectified by posting notices near cash register stations. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA L. PETERSON VS. US BANCORP DBA US BANK et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 2, 2012, Line 3, DEFENDANT U.S. BANCORP Notice Of Motion To Compel Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's Motion to Compel Further Responses to Form Interrogatories is granted in part, denied in part. In some instances there is no practical benefit to requiring more complete answers. Plaintiff shall supply answers or more complete answers to the following interrogatories: 2.12 section (c) only, 6.6, 6.7 section (a) only, 8.3, 8.4 as to how the amount was calculated only, 8.5, 8.7 as to how the amount was calculated only, 8.8, 9.2, 10.1, 10.3, 11.1, 12.1, 12.2, 12.3, 12.4, 12.6 requires yes or no answer only, 50.1 through 50.6 The motion is denied as to all other interrogatories. No sanctions for either party. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 2, 2012, Line 4, DEFENDANTs MILENA TALIAN, MISSION BAY HOUSING PTNRS, LP, FILLMORE MARKETPLACE, LP and RELATED MANAGEMENT COMPANY, LP'S Motion To Compel Responses To Defts Form Interrogatories, Set One and Request For Production Of Documents ;Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. rulings.Case Title: MAN YU GUAN VS. NATURAL WONDER COSMETICS LLC, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Natural Wonder Cosmetics Llc, Zhong Fu And Zhi Liu'S Motion To Reopen Discovery
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 2, 2012, Line 8, Defendants Natural Wonder Cosmetics Llc, Zhong Fu And Zhi Liu'S Motion To Reopen Discovery Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to reopen discovery is granted. Plaintiff has not submitted any admissible evidence in support of the contention that re-opening discovery would be prejudicial or would result in trial delay. Plaintiff shall respond to previously propounded written discovery by April 23, 2012. Defendants may take depositions up to and including May 7, 2012 which is the discovery cut-off in the related Wang matter. [Note: There is a discrepancy in the pleadings as to whether the cut off in Wang is May 7 or May 11]. Judicial economy suggests that the deposition cut-off here be slightly less than 30 days before the June 4, 2012 trial so that, where appropriate, depositions can be coordinated with the Wang matter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/hk

rulings.Case Title: JENNIFER NICOLETTO VS. MATTHEW D GRAMLY
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion For Order To Set Aside The Court'S Jan-30-12 Ruling, On The Basis Of Ccp 473 Or For Good Cause Pursuant To The Court'S Disctetion
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 2, 2012, Line 10, PLAINTIFF JENNIFER NICOLETTO'S Motion For Order To Set Aside The Court's Jan-30-12 Ruling, On The Basis Of CCP 473 Or For Good Cause Pursuant To The Court's Disctetion is Denied. This Court lacks jurisdiction to hear this motion, and lacked jurisdiction to hear the motion on January 30, 2012. This case was dismissed on July 27, 2011. A voluntary dismissal of an entire action deprives the court of both subject matter and personal jurisdiction. (Harris v. Billings (1993) 16 Cal.App.4th 1396, 1405; Sanabria v. Embrey (2001) 92 Cal.App.4th 422, 425; Gorgi v. Jack In The Box Inc. (2008) 166 Cal.App.4th 255, 261.) Any orders entered after dismissal are void. (Paniagua v. Orange County Fire Auth. (2007) 149 Cal.App.4th 83, 89). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: MORDECHAI D. PELTA et al VS. BRUCE M. MCCORMACK MD et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 2, 2012, Line 6, PLAINTIFF MORDECHAI PELTA, SARAH PELTA MOTION TO QUASH SERVICE OF SUMMONS OR STAY OR DISMISS Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion to quash subpoena of Dr. Pelta's records is granted. Dr. Pelta is a consultant, whose materials are entitled to qualified work product protection absent good cause justifying discovery. See Weil & Brown, California Civil Procedure Before Trial §§ 8:246 et seq. Defendants are not prejudiced by their inability to review these documents. If Dr. Pelta is later designated as an expert, his materials will be discoverable. Motion to quash subpoena of employment records granted in part and denied in part. Defendants shall be entitled to inspect documents related to wages and other employment earnings, they shall also be entitled to inspect documents relating to Mr. Pelta's physical condition and medical restrictions, if any. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK

rulings.Case Title: CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO VS. ANSHEN + ALLEN, ARCHITECTS, INC. et al*****TRANSFERRED TO ALAMEDA COUNTY*****
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order For The Preservation Of Evidence; Memo Of P & A; Dec And Pos
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, April 2, 2012, Line 7, DEFENDANT STANTEC ARCHITECTURE INC. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED AS, STANTEC CONSULTING SERVICES INC. (ERRONEOUSLY SUED Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order For The Preservation Of Evidence Pro Tem Judge Tom Cohen, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Department 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion for a protective order is denied. Defendant presented no declarations or other admissible evidence supporting the need for 14 days prior notice. CCSF shall provide a minimum of 5 business days or 10 calendar days prior notice of repairs. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to tom@thomascohen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/HK
